sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 15.88 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.15)

statement.latex: Let${ A}$and${ N}_c$denote the varieties of abelian groups and nilpotent groups of class$c$, respectively. Let$F_r= F_r({ A}{ N}_c)$be a free group of rank$r$in${ A}{ N}_c$. An element of$F_r$is called primitive\/ if it can be included in a basis of~$F_r$. Does there exist an algorithm recognizing primitive elements in~$F_r$? { E.\,I.\,Timoshenko}

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory IV_PROBLEM_UNDEFINED; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
